title: Shooting into a North Delta Residence
content:
Shooting into a North Delta Residence
Tuesday, Apr 7, 2026
News Release
Delta, BC – On April 3, 2026, at approximately 2:50 am, the Delta Police responded to a report of shots fired into a residence in the 11200 block of 92And avenue, Delta.
DPD officers arrived on scene and located shell casings and confirmed shots had been fired into a vacant basement suite. Fortunately, no one was injured in the occupied upper portion of the residence.
While the incident appears to be targeted, police are continuing to investigate the circumstances of the shooting; however, it does not appear to be extortion related. Investigators will be in the area canvassing for video evidence and speaking with witnesses.
Investigators are seeking any information or video footage at or around the time of the incident. Anyone with information, including CCTV or dashcam footage, who has not already spoken to police, is asked to call the Delta Police Department at 604-946-4444 or message
[email protected]
.

title: Motor Vehicle Collision
content:
Motor Vehicle Collision
Monday, Mar 9, 2026
News Release
Delta, BC –
Delta Police is advising of a fatal motor vehicle collision involving a pedestrian that occurred on March 6, 2026
in Delta.
At approximately 2:30 pm, Delta Police responded to a report of a motor vehicle collision in which a pedestrian was struck near 10
th
Ave and 54A Street in Tsawwassen. The collision occurred as a vehicle was entering a parking lot in the area.
A 69-year-old female pedestrian sustained serious injuries and, despite lifesaving efforts by first responders, later succumbed to those injuries
The vehicle involved remained at the scene, the driver is cooperating with investigators and impairment is not a factor. Delta Police continue to investigate the circumstances surrounding this collision.
Officers remain on scene, and residents are advised that road closures are in effect in the area of 10th Avenue and 53A Street through to 11th Avenue and 54A Street.
Anyone with more information, who has not yet spoken to Police, including those with dash cam footage, is asked to contact the Delta Police at 604-946-4411.

title: Small Plane Crashes in Burns Bog
content:
Small Plane Crashes in Burns Bog
Monday, Mar 2, 2026
News Release
Delta, BC – On February 28th, just after 10:30 am, the Delta Police was notified of a small plane that had crashed into Burns Bog. The pilot was the lone occupant, and suffered minor injuries. The pilot was transported to a local hospital for assessment.
The National Transportation Safety Board has been notified and will assume conduct of the ensuing investigation to determine cause. All media inquiries can be directed to the NTSB.
